question.title: How far will the Duke Blue Devils advance in the NCAA's 2026 March Madness?
question.description:
Duke University, in Durham, North Carolina, has [one of the most prolific men's college basketball teams](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Duke_Blue_Devils_men's_basketball) in the United States. Under the four-decade leadership of Coach Mike Krzyzewski, the Blue Devils were the number-one seed fourteen times, reached the Final Four thirteen times, and won the national championship five times, most recently [in 2015](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2015_NCAA_Division_I_men's_basketball_tournament).

Krzyzewski retired in 2022, but the team has continued to have success. Most recently, in 2025, Duke was the overall number-two seed going into [the tournament](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_NCAA_Division_I_men's_basketball_tournament), where it reached the Final Four before being knocked out by Houston.

The [2025–2026 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%9326_NCAA_Division_I_men's_basketball_season) has seen another strong showing from the Blue Devils, who as of late February have a 25-2 record and are [the number-one team in the country](https://www.espn.com/mens-college-basketball/rankings).

question.resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the furthest stage of the 2026 men's NCAA division I basketball tournament that the Duke Blue Devils qualify for.
question.fine_print:
If the tournament is canceled or the Duke Blue Devils do not qualify for the round of 64, this question will be annulled.

If Duke reaches the national championship game, the question will resolve according to whether Duke wins or loses that game.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42346) which opened on 2026-03-02 01:24:13. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print contained above. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
